observations: A lean mature humanoid creature crouches forward on bare clawed feet with long pointed ears, patchy fur and scarred skin, asymmetrical black hair, and two thin rat-like tails.
The viewer-right arm is unnaturally elongated toward the viewer and ends in five oversized translucent amber claws; this forced perspective makes the hand the immediate attack and identity cue.
The other arm stays tight across the abdomen while bent knees, forward shoulders, and the reaching hand create a stalking or snatching action.
Ragged layered wraps and dark cloth expose the bodily patchwork rather than presenting conventional armor.
defects: The extreme hand perspective makes the far arm and hand visually much larger than the torso, weakening certainty about the creature's resting proportions.
The dark clothing merges through the torso and hips, leaving the pale scarred limbs, ears, tails, and amber claws to carry most of the readable design.
The twin tails cross behind the extended arm and are easy to confuse with cords or tendrils.
uncertainties: The amber fingertip forms could be natural claws, containers, coated nails, or attached tools; the image does not resolve their mechanism.

observations: Three substantially different silhouettes are visible: a running upright thief carrying a large rounded object, a low rat-like sprinter with a long tail and greatly extended clawed arm, and a crouched figure under a broad triangular hood or cloak.
The middle candidate matches the master through the long ear, horizontal crouch, trailing tail, planted opposite hand, and long splayed reaching claws.
The alternatives change locomotion, body height, carried mass, and concealment rather than only changing limb pose.
defects: The selected middle silhouette's extended arm and claws dominate so strongly that the torso, supporting limbs, and second hand become secondary shapes.
Its long tail and thin claw tips are narrow features that can disappear when reduced.
uncertainties: The alternatives are not lettered or otherwise marked, so the selected status must be inferred from resemblance to the master.

observations: The crop shows one exposed amber eye fixed on the viewer, a raised inner brow, lifted cheek, asymmetrical closed-mouth smirk, and a head tilted forward beneath hair that hides the other eye.
The long pierced ear, pointed nose, facial scars, sparse fur, and visible small fangs establish a specific nonhuman face rather than a human with added ears.
The eye contact and compressed half-smile suggest sly appraisal or anticipated advantage, while the crouched shoulder keeps the expression connected to the reaching body action.
defects: Hair fully conceals one eye, limiting the visible eye-to-eye asymmetry to brow, cheek, and mouth.
The smirk can read as mischievous or playful as readily as predatory because the mouth and jaw show little tension.
uncertainties: The image does not distinguish whether the viewer is prey, an accomplice, or the owner of something being taken.

observations: At native size the crouched diagonal body, long pointed ear, reaching arm, and amber claw cluster remain separable.
The warm face and claw tips stand out against the nearly black clothing and background.
Facial scars, the smirk, patchy fur, garment layers, and most tail separation collapse below reliable recognition.
defects: The hand reads as a bright multi-pronged cluster, but the individual translucent claw construction is unclear.
The torso and rear leg merge into the background, reducing the body to face, forward foot, and reaching hand accents.
uncertainties: The thumbnail preserves a stalking reach but does not independently communicate whether the unit steals, poisons, slashes, or grabs.
